articles.title: Eight signings? De Zerbi's transfer plans to rebuild Tottenham this summer
articles.summary: Never again. That was the promise made by Tottenham chief executive Vinai Venkatesham and non-executive chairman Peter Charrington after narrowly avoiding relegation from the Premier League.

articles.title: 'Star in the making' - what is Man City's plan for forgotten man Reis?
articles.summary: Manchester City are still awaiting the arrival of their new manager but forgotten defender Vitor Reis will be back in the building following his season-long loan spell in La Liga.

articles.title: Wigan score 13 tries in York thrashing as Catalans and Castleford also win
articles.summary: Wigan Warriors scored 13 tries as they thumped York Knights 72-20 in the Betfred Super League with hat-tricks from Noah Hodkinson and Jai Field.

articles.title: The Super Bowl winner's son stepping into spotlight with USA
articles.summary: Alex Freeman is stepping out of the shadows of his Super Bowl-winning father to forge his own path in football.

articles.summary: BBC Sport explores how hydration break ads work in other countries and how they could impact the future of football.

articles.title: All three England keepers played there - Carlisle's role in trio's rise
articles.summary: From west Cumbria to the World Cup, Carlisle is England's goalkeeper factory, with all of Thomas Tuchel's goalkeepers passing through the club.

articles.title: Goalline technology denies Japan goal by fractions
articles.summary: Tunisia goalkeeper Aymen Dahmen makes an "unbelievable save" to prevent Japan from doubling their lead by the finest of margins, in their 2026 World Cup match at Estadio Monterrey.

articles.title: Impenetrable Room inspires Curacao to first World Cup point
articles.summary: Curacao goalkeeper Eloy Room equals the record for most saves in a World Cup match, as Dick Advocaat's side earn their first ever point in the tournament with a 0-0 draw against Ecuador at the Kansas ...

articles.title: History makers! Curacao earn first point, keeper records most saves at WC
articles.summary: Curacao goalkeeper Eloy Room made history with an incredible 15 saves to earn his country their first ever World Cup point by holding Ecuador to a 0-0 draw in Group E.

articles.title: Fastest World Cup to 100 goals in 68 years - are balls and breaks behind it?
articles.summary: The 2026 World Cup becomes the fastest edition of the tournament to hit 100 goals since 1958 - with the landmark reached in the 33rd game. But what is behind the scoring spree?

articles.title: Netherlands thrash Sweden to all but guarantee last-32 place
articles.summary: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo score two goals apiece as the Netherlands cruise past Sweden 5-1 to all but mathematically guarantee a place in the last 32 of the World Cup.

articles.title: Gakpo fires home the 100th goal of this World Cup
articles.summary: Cody Gakpo scores the 100th goal of this World Cup as he his second of the game to put Netherlands 4-0 ahead against Sweden.

articles.title: Saka trains alone as England prepare for Ghana game
articles.summary: Bukayo Saka was the only player not present in group training as England continued their preparations for their second World Cup match against Ghana.

articles.title: How does qualification for the World Cup knockout stage work?
articles.summary: With the second round of group-stage matches under way, we assess who can play who in the knockout rounds - and how they can get there.

articles.title: Brobbey scores twice against Sweden
articles.summary: Brian Brobbey scores twice in the first half in Netherland's World Cup match with Sweden.

articles.title: Cooling hoodies help England staff combat World Cup heat
articles.summary: As the England players trained under the searing heat of the Kansas City sun on Saturday, manager Thomas Tuchel took a very different approach to keeping himself cool.

articles.title: World Cup groups separated by head-to-head records for first time
articles.summary: Fifa is using head-to-head records instead of group goal difference as the first tiebreaker for teams who are level on points at the World Cup.

articles.title: Why are so many World Cup goals being scored late?
articles.summary: World Cup 2026 is witnessing a surge in late goals. Longer stoppage times, tactical substitutions and hydration breaks are reshaping matches
